title: Restore user data after draft expiration

requirements.text: Rebuild the full application (backend and frontend) with all existing data models and features intact: clients, interventions, user profiles, media/blob storage, technical folder, blacklist management, role-based access control, offline sync, and PWA support — restoring the app to its last known working state after the draft expired.

quotes: Remet met donné dans min appli

acceptanceCriteria: The app deploys and opens without crashing before the login screen.
Internet Identity authentication works and redirects to the home route after login.
The Clients page lists all clients with search, blacklist badges, and navigation to individual dossiers.
Client dossiers display contact info, blacklist status, and intervention history.
Interventions can be added, edited, deleted, and viewed with media attachments.
The Technical Folder page allows file upload, folder creation, renaming, moving, and viewing files.
The Dashboard page shows a calendar and interventions for the selected date.
Offline sync (outbox, sync engine, connectivity status bar) functions correctly.
PWA manifest and service worker are present and the app is installable.
Role-based access control is enforced on the backend.
Data export (JSON/PDF) dialog is accessible and functional.

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in a single actor in backend/main.mo.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Preserve the existing brand color theme (Vial Traite Service) defined in frontend/src/index.css.
nonGoals: Adding new features beyond restoring the existing application state.
Changing the authentication provider (Internet Identity must remain).
Migrating to a different frontend framework or backend language.
